summary: Slays Wen Chou at the Yan Crossing (Yan Jin) when the latter's column collides with Cao Cao's column at the ford. Receives word via a recovered letter that Liu Bei lives at Yuan Shao's court, returns the gifts Cao Cao gave him, hangs his seal of office on the gatepost, and rides off with Liu Bei's wives - the start of his solitary journey.
Wen Chou; Yuan Shao's second great champion; routs Cao Cao's vanguard at Yan Crossing before Guan Yu charges in and cuts him down with a single saber-stroke. The mirror of Yan Liang's death.
Loses Wen Chou's value to Guan Yu and then loses Guan Yu himself - refuses Cheng Yu's advice to send pursuit. 'Each has his master; do not pursue.' The chapter's reading of his magnanimity.

Liu Bei; petitions Yuan Shao that the red-faced warrior killing Yuan Shao's champions cannot be his brother; Yuan Shao believes him. Writes a secret letter to Guan Yu via Sun Qian that triggers Guan Yu's departure.
Yuan Shao; threatens Liu Bei with death after Wen Chou's killing, then accepts Liu Bei's explanation and reverses again. The chapter portrays his vacillation.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Ju Shou; warns Yuan Shao before Yan Crossing - 'do not cross the river yet'; ignored, then partly vindicated.
Yan Liang; named in passing - already dead at Baima.
Sun Qian; carries Liu Bei's letter from Jizhou to Guan Yu in Xuchang - the message that brings Guan Yu out.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Liu Pi; Runan-based Yellow Turban remnant chief; received as Yuan Shao's southern arm and the place Liu Bei plans to relocate to.
Gong Du; Liu Pi's partner at Runan; the second leader of the rebel forces Liu Bei eventually joins.
Chen Zhen; Yuan Shao's envoy who carries the Yuan Shao-Sun Ce alliance overture.
Shen Pei; with Yuan Shao at the war council.
Guo Tu; with Yuan Shao at the war council.
Cai Yang; Cao Cao officer; pursues Guan Yu over his nephew Qin Qi's death - the open-ended pursuit that will close at Gucheng.
Cao Hong; with Cao Cao at the Yellow River frontier.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Xiahou Dun; commander at the Yellow River frontier.
Xu Huang; commander at the Yellow River frontier; engages Wen Chou before Guan Yu's strike.
Yu Jin; commander at the Yellow River frontier.
Zhang Liao; serves under Cao Cao.
Xun You; with Cao Cao.
Xun Yu; running Xuchang.
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Lady Gan; protected by Guan Yu in the carriage as he sets out from Xuchang.

The 'Solitary Journey' chapter - escorts Liu Bei's wives north toward Yuan Shao's territory. At each of the five passes (Dongling, Luoyang, Sishui, Xingyang, Yellow River) refuses to wait for Cao Cao's pass-edict and forces the issue with the saber. Kills six gate-commanders in succession: Kong Xiu, Meng Tan, Han Fu (this Han Fu is a gate captain - not the Jizhou one), Bian Xi, Wang Zhi, Qin Qi. Eventually crosses the Yellow River.

Sends Zhang Liao after Guan Yu with a fighting robe, gold for the road, and the pass-edict that should have spared Guan Yu the bloodshed at the five passes - the edict arrives one pass late at each, by Cao Cao's character-revealing design.
Zhang Liao; rides after Guan Yu carrying the robe and gold; conveys Cao Cao's farewell.

Liao Hua's debut - Yellow Turban remnant bandit who offers his services to Guan Yu and Liu Bei's wives but is politely refused (Guan Yu doesn't want to mix his column with bandits). Will reappear under Liu Bei's banner later.
Wang Zhi; commander at the fifth pass who tries to burn Guan Yu out at night with a treacherous welcome; killed by Guan Yu when his guard Hu Ban warns the visitor.
Wang Zhi (duplicate record).
Cai Yang; continues to demand permission to pursue Guan Yu; Cao Cao keeps refusing; will catch up at Gucheng.
Liu Bei; off-stage at Yuan Shao's camp; will be reached after the river crossing.
Cheng Yu; argues for pursuit; Cao Cao refuses.
Cheng Yu (duplicate record).
Gong Du; named in passing in the recap of Liu Bei's destination.
Liu Pi; named in passing as Liu Bei's destination after he leaves Yuan Shao.
Ju Shou; with Yuan Shao.
Sun Qian; with Liu Bei at Yuan Shao's camp.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Tian Feng; still jailed at Yuan Shao's court.
Li Dian; named in passing in Cao Cao's circle.
Xiahou Dun; named in passing - soon to pursue Guan Yu in chapter 28.
Xu Chu; Cao Cao's bodyguard captain at court when Guan Yu leaves.
Xu Huang; named in passing.
Yan Liang; named in passing - long dead.
Wen Chou; named in passing - long dead.
Yu Jin; named in passing.
Yuan Shao; off-stage at Jizhou.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).

Approaches Gucheng where Zhang Fei has just installed himself; Zhang Fei doesn't believe his loyalty until he kills Cai Yang in a single saber-stroke before the wine grows cold. Reunion at Gucheng - the long arc since Xuzhou closes.
Zhang Fei; has seized Gucheng during Guan Yu's exile; tries to attack Guan Yu on suspicion of defection; demands the brother prove loyalty by killing Cai Yang. Once it's done, embraces him.
Cai Yang; finally catches up to Guan Yu at Gucheng demanding revenge for his nephew Qin Qi; killed by Guan Yu in a single saber-stroke. The proof Zhang Fei demanded.
Reunites with both brothers, Zhao Yun, Mi Zhu, Mi Fang, and Sun Qian at Gucheng - a council that returns the brothers to Runan to begin again under Liu Pi and Gong Du.
Zhao Yun; arrives at Gucheng with Liu Bei - the reunion of all of Liu Bei's core lieutenants under a single banner for the first time in many chapters.
Mi Zhu; arrives at Gucheng with Liu Bei.
Mi Fang; arrives at Gucheng with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian; arrives at Gucheng with Liu Bei.
Sun Qian (duplicate record).
Jian Yong; arrives at Gucheng with Liu Bei.
Guan Ping's debut - Guan Yu's adopted son, taken in on the road from a family that wishes to follow him. Will be one of Guan Yu's closest companions and die with him at Maicheng.
Liao Hua; again offers his services to Guan Yu, this time accepted in passing as the brothers return south.
Off-stage in this chapter - informed of Guan Yu's reunion with his brothers.
Liu Biao; named in passing in the regional roll.
Liu Pi; receives Liu Bei back at Runan after the reunion.
Gong Du; with Liu Pi at Runan.
Shentu Pan; named in passing in the Gongsun Zan recap.
Gongsun Zan; named in passing in the recap of Zhao Yun's old service.
Guo Chang; Yuan Shao officer named in passing.
Guo Huai; named in passing - will be a major Wei general later.
Guan Ning; minor figure named in passing.
Chen Zhen; named in passing as Yuan Shao's envoy.
Guo Tu; with Yuan Shao.
Yuan Shao; off-stage; Liu Bei has slipped out of his camp.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Yan Liang; named in passing in the recap.
Wen Chou; named in passing in the recap.
Zhang Jue; named in passing - Liao Hua's old Yellow Turban roots.
Zhang Liao; named in passing in Cao Cao's circle.
Xiahou Dun; pursues Guan Yu briefly before Cao Cao's pass-edict reaches him.
Lady Gan; restored to Liu Bei at Gucheng.
Lady Mi; restored to Liu Bei at Gucheng.
Sun Ce; named in passing - about to die in chapter 29.

Sun Ce dies aged twenty-six. After publicly executing the Daoist Yu Ji and refusing to acknowledge the wound dealt by Xu Gong's three retainers in the hunting forest, his face wounds open during a final glance in the mirror. Names Sun Quan his successor with the line 'for internal matters consult Zhang Zhao; for external Zhou Yu' before dying.

Sun Quan; eighteen years old; succeeds Sun Ce as the lord of the South Land. Receives the deathbed mandate; weeps over his brother; immediately begins forming his own counsel circle with Zhou Yu's help.

Zhou Yu; arrives at Chaisang from the western front in time for Sun Ce's funeral; receives the formal mandate to manage external affairs and brings in Lu Su, Zhuge Jin, and others to staff Sun Quan's new court.

Lu Su's debut - recruited by Zhou Yu; presents Sun Quan with the first comprehensive plan for the South Land (very similar to Zhuge Liang's later Longzhong Plan): secure the Yangtze, take Jingzhou, then contest the empire.
Zhuge Jin's debut - Zhuge Liang's elder brother; recruited by Sun Quan as adviser through Lu Su.
Gu Yong; recruited by Zhang Hong's recommendation as Sun Quan's deputy administrator.
Hua Xin; sent by Cao Cao to Sun Quan as a peace envoy after Sun Ce's death; carries the imperial recognition that confirms Sun Quan's rule.

Xu Gong; Governor of Wujun whose secret letter to Cao Cao denouncing Sun Ce is intercepted. Sun Ce strangles him in the council hall - and three of his retainers later wait in the hunting forest to take revenge.
Hua Tuo; sent for by the dying Sun Ce; pronounces him untreatable due to the poison-arrow wound's spread.
Lady Wu; Sun Ce and Sun Quan's mother; presides over Sun Ce's deathbed and ratifies the succession.
Cheng Pu; senior general at Sun Ce's death; pledges loyalty to Sun Quan.
Han Dang; senior general at Sun Ce's death.
Sun Jing; Sun Quan's uncle; advises through the succession.
Sun Kuang; Sun Quan's younger brother; named in the succession arrangements.
Sun Fen; Sun-family member named in passing during the succession.
Liu Wan; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Xu; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Zhao; civil administrator named in Sun Ce's deathbed mandate ('for internal matters consult Zhang Zhao').
Zhang Zhao (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong; one of the two Zhangs at Sun Quan's court.
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Zhang Hong (duplicate record).
Yu Fan; civil officer at Sun Quan's court.
Lu Fan; serves at Sun Quan's court.
Lu Fan (duplicate record).
Cao Cao; promotes Sun Quan to General Who Conquers the Caitiffs after Sun Ce's death - formal recognition of the southern regime to keep it neutral during the Guandu showdown.
Cao Ren; named in passing in Cao Cao's command.
Guo Jia; advises Cao Cao to recognise Sun Quan to prevent a southern second front.
Liu Ye; among Cao Cao's advisers.
Huang Zu; Sun Ce's nemesis at Xiakou - named in passing as the unfinished business Sun Quan inherits.
Liu Biao; named in passing in the southern strategic roll.
Yuan Shao; off-stage at the Yellow River; about to lose Guandu.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Chen Zhen; carries Yuan Shao's overtures to Sun Quan; finds Sun Ce's regime in mourning.
Cai Yong; named in passing in a literary aside about the south's cultural lineage.
Zhang Jing; minor figure named in passing.
Zhang Jing (duplicate record).
Zheng Bao; minor figure named in passing.
Liu Xiu; opening-frame reference to the Latter Han founder.

Battle of Guandu chapter - outnumbered seven to one, holds the line until Xu You defects from Yuan Shao with intelligence about Wuchao. Burns the Yuan grain depot at Wuchao under cover of night with Xu Chu and Xiahou Dun; the resulting collapse of Yuan Shao's army wins Cao Cao the north. The defining victory of his career.

Yuan Shao; rejects Tian Feng's counsel from prison, Ju Shou's counsel in the field, and Xu You's counsel at the camp - leading him to lose the war entirely. Flees north with only 800 horsemen after Wuchao burns; the chapter's portrait of strategic self-destruction.
Yuan Shao (duplicate record).
Xu You; defects to Cao Cao mid-campaign after Shen Pei has his family imprisoned over a corruption charge. Tells Cao Cao the location of the Wuchao grain depot - the intelligence that wins Guandu.
Xu You (duplicate record).
Xu You (duplicate record).

Chunyu Qiong; commander of the Wuchao depot; drunk when Cao Cao's raid arrives. Captured by Yue Jin and brought before Cao Cao who cuts off his ears, nose and fingers and sends him back to Yuan Shao on a horse.

Ju Shou; warns Yuan Shao not to commit to pitched battle; ignored. Captured at Guandu, refuses to surrender to Cao Cao, attempts to flee back to Yuan Shao on a stolen horse and is executed. Cao Cao buries him with full honours.

Tian Feng; still in prison at Jizhou. Forecasts Yuan Shao's defeat correctly. When Yuan Shao returns broken, Tian Feng is put to death by Peng Ji's whispered slander rather than acknowledge his prophecy.
Shen Pei; the corrupt jailing of Xu You's family that drives Xu You's defection.
Guo Tu; encourages Yuan Shao toward the disastrous open-field battle; later shifts blame to Zhang He and Gao Lan, driving them to defect to Cao Cao.
Zhang He; defects to Cao Cao with Gao Lan when Guo Tu poisons Yuan Shao against them - Cao Cao welcomes him and his career as one of the great Wei generals begins.
Gao Lan; defects to Cao Cao alongside Zhang He.

Jia Xu; advises Cao Cao through Guandu - at the council that decides whether to retreat (Cao Cao was nearly out of grain), Jia Xu's '500 lieu-pounds of grain is enough, the day will turn' line stiffens Cao Cao's resolve.
Jia Xu (duplicate record).
Xun You; advises the Wuchao raid; named with Jia Xu as Cao Cao's key strategists at Guandu.
Xun Yu; sends the famous letter from Xuchang stiffening Cao Cao's resolve when he was on the verge of pulling back ('the small holds the great here - do not falter').
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Xun Yu (duplicate record).
Cao Hong; commands the Cao Cao camp while the Wuchao raid is underway.
Cao Hong (duplicate record).
Cao Ren; commands part of the Cao Cao army at Guandu.
Xu Chu; rides with Cao Cao on the Wuchao night raid as bodyguard.
Xiahou Dun; commands the Cao Cao van at Guandu.
Xiahou Yuan; commands the Cao Cao van at Guandu.
Xu Huang; rides with Cao Cao on the Wuchao raid.
Yu Jin; commands part of the Cao Cao army at Guandu.
Li Dian; commands part of the Cao Cao army at Guandu.
Zhang Liao; commands part of the Cao Cao army at Guandu.
Liu Ye; designs the 'lightning-arrow' carts that destroy Yuan Shao's siege-towers.
Shi Huan; minor figure named in passing.
Han Meng; one of Yuan Shao's officers tasked with moving Wuchao grain - captured during the Cao Cao raid.
Han Juzi; minor Yuan Shao officer named in passing.
Wei Zi; minor figure named in passing.
Yuan Tan; Yuan Shao's eldest son; rallies remnants after Wuchao.
Yuan Shang; Yuan Shao's third son; favoured for succession; arrives with reinforcements during the campaign.
Zhao Rui; minor figure named in passing.
Dong Zhuo; named in passing in the chapter's recap.
